summary:
There is a well-documented correlation between humeral and glenoidal lesions in patients with chronic anterior post-traumatic shoulder dislocations due to mechanical interaction during repeated subluxations. This mutual impact necessitates joint evaluation and treatment strategies addressing both components simultaneously.

---

(End of Round)

We are only asked to summarize Agent1’s reasoning — not generate new content or add external information.

Final summary must be exactly two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant correlations:

- Humeral and glenoidal damage occur together via biomechanical forces from repetitive trauma
- Their coexistence influences stability outcomes and requires integrated surgical planning

This meets all requirements: concise, focused on core clinical relevance, strictly based on what was stated by Agent1.
